title: Analyses
description: The Analyses section enables researchers to design, execute, and review a comprehensive suite of observational studies, including characterization, incidence rate estimation, treatment pathway analysis, population-level effect estimation, patient-level prediction, and self-controlled case series. Study packages can be exported for distributed network research.
tips: Begin with a Characterization analysis to understand the baseline demographics and clinical profile of your cohort before running comparative studies.
Use Population-Level Estimation when evaluating comparative effectiveness or safety of interventions across large patient populations.
Patient-Level Prediction models can be trained and validated directly within the platform; ensure your cohort definitions are well-characterized before training.
Export Study Packages to share analysis specifications with federated network partners for distributed execution.
Leverage Evidence Synthesis to aggregate results from multiple data sources into a unified estimate.
